RECOUP Integrative, Root-Cause Oral Health Program

Why Oral Health Matters

Your mouth is more than just a smile—it's a gateway to your overall health. Poor oral hygiene can lead to gum disease (periodontitis), which doesn't just affect your teeth and gums—it can contribute to heart disease, diabetes, Alzheimer's, and more.

The good news is that you can keep your mouth and body healthy with proper oral care, nutrition, and lifestyle choices!

Common Oral Health Issues and their Impact

  • Gum Disease (Periodontitis) – Affects 75-80% of adults in India, often without symptoms. It's linked to heart disease, diabetes, pregnancy complications, and even cognitive decline.
  • Tooth Decay and Cavities – Caused by bacteria and poor oral hygiene, leading to infections, pain, and even tooth loss.
  • Bad Breath (Halitosis) – Often due to poor oral hygiene, gut imbalances, or underlying health conditions.
  • Dry Mouth and oral Infections Can increase the risk of decay and gum disease, sometimes caused by medications or dehydration.
  • Mercury exposure from amalgam fillings may be linked to conditions such as chronic fatigue syndrome, autoimmune disorders, and neurological issues.
  • Root canal procedures may be associated with cardiovascular diseases, systemic infections, and autoimmune conditions.

The Link Between Oral Health and Systemic Diseases

Research shows that oral bacteria can enter the bloodstream, triggering chronic inflammation that contributes to:

  • Heart Disease and Stroke – Oral bacteria can damage arteries and increase plaque buildup.
  • Diabetes – Poor gum health worsens blood sugar control and vice versa.
  • Alzheimer's and Cognitive Decline – Chronic inflammation from gum disease is linked to brain ageing.
  • Pregnancy Complications – Increases the risk of preterm birth and low birth weight.
  • Autoimmune Conditions and Gut Health – Poor oral health can disrupt the gut microbiome, affecting irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), arthritis, and immune function.

How to Keep Your Mouth and Body Healthy

1. Daily Oral Hygiene Habits 🪥

  • ✔ Brush twice a day with fluoride-free toothpaste (look for xylitol or charcoal-based options).
  • ✔ Floss daily to remove bacteria between teeth.
  • ✔ Use a tongue scraper to reduce bacterial buildup.
  • ✔ Rinse with an antibacterial mouthwash (avoid alcohol-based options).

2. Nutrition for a Healthy Mouth 🥦

Foods to Eat:

  • ✅ Leafy greens, nuts, and seeds for vitamins and minerals.
  • ✅ Probiotic-rich foods like yoghurt and fermented foods for a balanced oral microbiome.
  • ✅ Fatty fish and walnuts for Omega-3s, which help reduce gum inflammation.
  • ✅ Berries, green tea, and turmeric for their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.

Foods to Avoid:

  • ❌ Excess sugar and processed foods that fuel harmful bacteria.
  • ❌ Acidic drinks like soda and energy drinks that erode enamel.
  • ❌ Smoking and alcohol increase the risk of gum disease and dry mouth.

3. Boost Your Oral and Overall Health

  • 🔹 Hydrogen Peroxide Flossing can help with gum inflammation and whitening (use for short periods).
  • 🔹 Oil pulling can reduce harmful bacteria and freshen breath.
  • 🔹 Probiotics support a healthy oral microbiome.
  • 🔹 Supplements help strengthen gums and fight inflammation.

4. Address Underlying Health Issues

  • 🔸 Gum Bleeding or Sensitivity? Check for micronutrient deficiencies.
  • 🔸 Frequent Cavities? Consider gut health imbalances and high sugar intake.
  • 🔸 Snoring or Sleep Apnea? Your oral health could be contributing to it! Ask about a mouthguard or sleep assessment.

5. Regular Checkups and Preventive Care

  • 🔹 See a functional dentist or periodontist every 6 months for a deep cleaning and early detection of issues.
  • 🔹 If you have chronic health conditions (diabetes, heart disease, or autoimmune disorders), discuss how oral health impacts your overall treatment plan.

Take Control of Your Oral and Whole-Body Health!

Your oral health is the foundation of your overall well-being. You can prevent disease and feel your best by making small adjustments to your oral care, diet, and lifestyle!

➡ Need personalized guidance? Book a consultation today and take charge of your health.
